recipe.description:
I kind of merge the two recipes together

less than 1/2 cup vinegar
honey is bomb
cook chicken separately so it gets rid of it's grease
learn how to cook/reduce gravy

cinnamon and brown sugar/honey are secret ingredients
recipe.notes: Tags: Filipino, Main, Normal, chicken thighs, coconut milk, cauliflower
recipe.title: Chicken Adobo

ingredient_lines.raw_text: 1/2 cup (125 ml) white Filipino cane vinegar, or distilled white vinegar

ingredient_lines.preparation: smashed with the side of a knife and peeled
ingredient_lines.raw_text: 6–8 cloves garlic, smashed with the side of a knife and peeled
½ head of cauliflower, broken into small florets
¼ kabocha squash, cut into 1-inch pieces (about 2 cups)
1 cup sugarcane vinegar or distilled white vinegar, divided
4 chicken legs, drumsticks and thighs separated

ingredient_lines.preparation: Unsalted, roasted, thinly sliced, and sliced
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Unsalted, roasted pumpkin seeds (pepitas), thinly sliced Fresno chiles, and sliced scallion (for serving)

ingredient_lines.note: in center of cheesecloth and tie closed with kitchen twine; set sachet aside. Toast turmeric in a dry small skillet over medium-low heat, just until fragrant (be careful not to let it brown), about 3 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Place bay leaves and peppercorns in center of cheesecloth and tie closed with kitchen twine; set sachet aside. Toast turmeric in a dry small skillet over medium-low heat, stirring often, just until fragrant (be careful not to let it brown), about 3 minutes. Transfer to a plate.

ingredient_lines.note: in same skillet over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until burnt (not browned: burnt), about 3 minutes., Transfer to another plate.
ingredient_lines.preparation: Grind in a spice mill or with a mortar and pestle or finely chop.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Cook coconut in same skillet over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until burnt (not browned: burnt), about 3 minutes. Grind in a spice mill or with a mortar and pestle or finely chop. Transfer to another plate.

ingredient_lines.note: Heat oven to 375°. Toss cauliflower and squash on a large rimmed baking sheet with 1 Tbsp oil to coat; season with salt and pepper., tossing occasionally, 30-40 minutes.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Heat oven to 375°. Toss cauliflower and squash on a large rimmed baking sheet with 1 Tbsp. oil to coat; season with salt and pepper. Roast vegetables, tossing occasionally, until browned and tender, 30–40 minutes.

ingredient_lines.note: over medium-high., stirring often, until golden brown and very fragrant, about 1 minute. Stir in toasted turmeric, and 3/4 cup vinegar.
ingredient_lines.preparation: 8-10 minutes., stirring often, just until fragrant, Bring to a boil and cook until liquid is reduced by about one-third, 20-30 minutes.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Heat remaining 2 Tbsp. oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium-high. Add onion, shallots, garlic, and ginger and cook, stirring often, until golden brown and very fragrant, 8–10 minutes. Add red pepper flakes and cook, stirring often, just until fragrant, about 1 minute. Stir in toasted turmeric, coconut milk, and ¾ cup vinegar. Bring to a boil and cook until liquid is reduced by about one-third, 20–30 minutes.

ingredient_lines.note: prepare a grill for medium-high heat (or heat a grill pan over medium-high). Season chicken with salt and pepper and grill, turning occasionally, (chicken will not be cooked through at this point), 8-10 minutes.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Meanwhile, prepare a grill for medium-high heat (or heat a grill pan over medium-high). Season chicken with salt and pepper and grill, turning occasionally, just until skin is charred (chicken will not be cooked through at this point), 8–10 minutes.

ingredient_lines.note: Add chicken and reserved sachet to turmeric sauce. Cook, until chicken is tender, 60-80 minutes. Stir honey and remaining 1/4 cup vinegar in a small bowl until honey is dissolved; add to braise, then add roasted cauliflower and squash. Taste and season with more salt or honey as needed.
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Add chicken and reserved sachet to turmeric sauce. Cook, partially covered, until chicken is tender, 60–80 minutes. Stir honey and remaining ¼ cup vinegar in a small bowl until honey is dissolved; add to braise, then add roasted cauliflower and squash. Taste and season with more salt or honey as needed.

ingredient_lines.input_item: Divide adobo among bowls and top with burnt coconut
ingredient_lines.raw_text: Divide adobo among bowls and top with burnt coconut, pumpkin seeds, chiles, and scallion.

steps.instruction: Place the soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, black peppercorns, and bay leaves in a large, nonreactive sauté pan, and then nestle the chicken thighs, skin side down, into the pan. Bring the liquid to a boil over high heat, and then cover and simmer over low heat for 20 minutes. Turn the chicken over, and then cover and simmer for another 10 minutes.

Uncover the pan, and then increase the heat to high and return the sauce to a boil. While occasionally turning and basting the chicken, continue boiling the sauce, uncovered, until it is reduced by half and thickens slightly, 5–7 minutes. Serve with steamed white rice.
Variations: While the sauce is reducing, transfer the chicken thighs, skin side up, to a foil-lined sheet pan. Brown the chicken thighs underneath the broiler for 3–5 minutes.
Use freshly ground black pepper instead of whole peppercorns.

For a “drier” chicken adobo, you can reduce the sauce until it is almost completely evaporated. The chicken will then begin to fry in its own fat that is still left in the pan. This is how my grandmother finishes her adobo.
For a saucier adobo, double the amount of soy sauce and vinegar.
